meaning: adj.独立式的,不需依靠支撑物的
examples: Freestanding kivas discovered in the ruins of European-style missionary communities have been explained by some historians as examples of "superposition".
A Moscow company is now marketing "Sleepboxes"—freestanding, mobile boxes with beds inside—for travelers stranded overnight, or those in need of a quick snooze.
Freestanding in parallel alignment, the interior walls never meet the enclosure.
Suites have bright, freestanding bathtubs and the eighth-floor chill-out lounge opens to a panoramic sun deck.
There is a freestanding pub with billiards, a library, even a traditional Japanese bathhouse.
